items.title: Flat vs src layout
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  subgraph Flat["Flat layout"]
    F1["project/"] --> F2["ragapp/"]
    F1 --> F3["tests/"]
    F1 --> F4["pyproject.toml"]
  end
  subgraph Src["src layout"]
    S1["project/"] --> S2["src/"]
    S2 --> S3["ragapp/"]
    S1 --> S4["tests/"]
    S1 --> S5["pyproject.toml"]
  end
items.caption: Same project, two layouts. In `src/`, the package is physically inside `src/`, isolated from project metadata.

items.title: Why src/ forces installation
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  CWD["cwd = project/"] --> Path["sys.path includes project/"]
  Path --> FlatImp["Flat: ragapp/ is on sys.path → import works without install"]
  Path --> SrcImp["src: src/ragapp/ NOT on sys.path → ImportError unless installed"]
  SrcImp --> Install["pip install -e ."]
  Install --> Works["import ragapp works (against installed dist)"]
items.caption: `src/` removes the ambient import. The only path to `import ragapp` is through the installer, which is exactly how end users will reach it.
